vocabulary.definition_ar: قديم جداً، من زمن بعيد في التاريخ
vocabulary.example_sentence: The ancient pyramids are very old buildings in Egypt.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يجذب، يستقطب الناس للمجيء أو الزيارة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The new shopping mall attracts many customers.
She carefully walks on the wet floor.
Please drive carefully in the rain.
I need to complete my homework before dinner.
They constructed a new school in our neighborhood.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يستمر، يواصل فعل شيء بدون توقف
vocabulary.example_sentence: I will continue studying English every day.
The teacher will demonstrate how to solve the math problem.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مفصّل، يحتوي على تفاصيل كثيرة، دقيق
vocabulary.example_sentence: The teacher gave us a detailed explanation of the lesson.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ضخم جداً، كبير جداً في الحجم
vocabulary.example_sentence: The elephant is an enormous animal.
The football match was very exciting.
The weather is extremely hot today.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مشهور، معروف من قبل كثير من الناس
vocabulary.example_sentence: Paris is a famous city in France.
The movie was incredible - I loved it!
The king ruled for many years.

vocabulary.definition_ar: رائع، عظيم، جميل جداً ومثير للإعجاب
vocabulary.example_sentence: The palace has a magnificent garden with beautiful flowers.
Pizza is a popular food around the world.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حَمى، حافظ على، وقى من الضرر
vocabulary.example_sentence: The umbrella protected me from the rain.
The queen wore a gold crown.

vocabulary.definition_ar: فجأة، بشكل مفاجئ، بسرعة وبدون تحذير
vocabulary.example_sentence: It suddenly started to rain while we were walking.
Thousands of people came to the festival.
Buses transport students to school every day.
They found a treasure in the sand.
The sword was an old weapon.
The chariot was pulled by horses.
The king sat on the throne.
The empire collapsed in the end.
The mummy is in the museum.

paragraphs: Deep under the desert sand and jungle leaves, *ancient* cities wait for discovery. These lost places tell amazing stories about people who lived thousands of years ago. Archaeologists are scientists who *carefully* dig up these old cities to learn about past civilizations.

One of the most *famous* ancient cities is Pompeii in Italy. In 79 AD, a volcano *suddenly* covered this Roman city with ash and rock. The ash *protected* the city for almost 2,000 years. Today, visitors can walk through Pompeii's streets and see houses, shops, and even food that people left behind. The city shows us exactly how Romans lived, worked, and ate their meals.

In Cambodia, the *magnificent* temple city of Angkor Wat *attracts* millions of visitors every year. Built in the 12th century, this *enormous* stone city was once the center of the Khmer Empire. The temples cover 400 square kilometers - that's bigger than many modern cities! Kings and thousands of workers spent 30 years building these beautiful temples with *detailed* stone carvings.

Archaeologists *continue* to find new ancient cities around the world. In 2018, scientists discovered a huge Maya city in Guatemala using special airplane technology. This city had 60,000 buildings hidden under thick jungle trees. Each new discovery helps us understand how ancient people built their homes, grew their food, and created their art.

These ancient civilizations teach us that people have always been creative and intelligent. They built incredible cities, created beautiful art, and developed smart ways to solve problems. Their stories remind us that human civilization has a long and fascinating history.

The Great Pyramid of Giza in Egypt is one of the most *incredible* structures ever built by humans. This ancient wonder was *constructed* over 4,500 years ago for Pharaoh Khufu, and it remained the world's tallest building for almost 4,000 years. The pyramid stands 146 meters high and contains approximately 2.3 million stone blocks.

Building the pyramids required *thousands* of workers who labored for many years. These skilled builders had to *transport* massive stone blocks from quarries located far away. Each block weighed between 2 to 15 tons. Workers used wooden rollers, ramps, and ropes to move these heavy stones. They worked *carefully* to place each block in exactly the right position.

The construction process was *extremely* complex and required advanced planning. Ancient Egyptian engineers designed the pyramids with mathematical precision. The base of the Great Pyramid covers 13 acres of land, and each side is perfectly aligned with the four directions: north, south, east, and west. Scientists believe it took approximately 20 years to *complete* this magnificent structure.

Today, archaeologists continue to make *exciting* discoveries about pyramid construction. Recent studies using modern technology have revealed hidden chambers inside the Great Pyramid. These findings help us understand how ancient Egyptians achieved such remarkable engineering without modern machines. The pyramids *demonstrate* the incredible skills and knowledge of these ancient civilizations.

Visiting the pyramids remains a *popular* experience for millions of tourists each year. Standing before these ancient monuments, visitors can appreciate the dedication and expertise of the builders who created these lasting symbols of human achievement thousands of years ago.

sections.content_ar: نستخدم How many مع الأسماء القابلة للعد، و How much مع الأسماء غير القابلة للعد
sections.content_en:
How Much and How Many are used to ask about quantity.

Examples:
• How many pyramids are in Egypt? - There are many pyramids in Egypt.
• How much gold did the pharaohs have? - They had a lot of gold.
• How many people lived in ancient Rome? - Millions of people lived there.

The Rule:
Use HOW MANY with countable nouns (things you can count: 1, 2, 3...)
Use HOW MUCH with uncountable nouns (things you cannot count individually)

Countable: pyramids, temples, soldiers, kings
Uncountable: gold, water, food, time, money

sections.content:
How many + countable noun + ?
How much + uncountable noun + ?

items.sentence: How many temples did the Romans build?
How much silver was used in ancient coins?
items.translation_ar: كم من الفضة استُخدمت في النقود القديمة؟

items.sentence: How many gods did the Egyptians worship?

items.explanation_ar: الأهرامات قابلة للعد، لذا نستخدم How many

items.wrong: How many gold did they find?
items.correct: How much gold did they find?
items.explanation_ar: الذهب غير قابل للعد، لذا نستخدم How much
الجنود قابلون للعد، لذا نستخدم How many
